Display',-apple-system-headline,system-ui,-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,'Segoe UI',Roboto,Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif,'Apple Color Emoji','Segoe UI Emoji','Segoe UI Symbol';font-weight: bold;-webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;-moz-osx-font-smoothing: antialiased;-webkit-appearance: optimizelegibility;-moz-appearance: optimizelegibility;appearance: optimizelegibility;margin: 1em 0 0.625em 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 1.16em;font-size: 2em;">Act II. The Bailout That Isn’t a Bailout</h1><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">Anyone who thinks Anthropic brought this upon itself has forgotten the third player: OpenAI with its CEO Sam Altman.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><strong>Unlike Anthropic, OpenAI is in serious trouble. </strong><span>The company that launched the LLM hype with the massive success of ChatGPT has become the central node in a web of circular financing deals that </span><a href="https://substack.com/redirect/d5ca7987-62ca-47e7-92cd-44d501506b18?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg" rel="" style="color: rgb(54,55,55);text-decoration: underline;">artificially prop up large parts</a><span> of the US tech economy. When Microsoft “invests” in OpenAI only for that money to flow directly back as payment for Azure services, what emerges can hardly be described as value chain as it mimicks a carousel. That such constructions blur the line between creative accounting and antitrust evasion </span><a href="https://substack.com/redirect/06c1f260-657f-49de-aebb-5152f1390a1f?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg" rel="" style="color: rgb(54,55,55);text-decoration: underline;">is no secret</a><span>.</span></p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><span>And as tech critic Ed Zitron recently revealed through leaked financial documents, the subsequent revenue numbers that OpenAI has shown to the world are failing to convince anyone that the ‘</span><a href="https://substack.com/redirect/28a86548-9cda-463c-9f81-53c7c73c3bac?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg" rel="" style="color: rgb(54,55,55);text-decoration: underline;">math is mathing</a><span>’.</span></p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><span>The deals have grown so vast that OpenAI is already being </span><a href="https://substack.com/redirect/22817f9c-44d6-4813-bdd6-de2977b92039?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg" rel="" style="color: rgb(54,55,55);text-decoration: underline;">labeled</a><span> “too big to fail” — and the question of a state-guaranteed bailout hangs openly in the air. Set against Anthropic’s success — better products, no absurd debt mountains, everything done “correctly” — OpenAI’s position turns precarious.</span></p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">When customers began defecting en masse to Google and flagship model underperformed, the company declared “Code Red” in December 2025 — halting most side projects overnight in a desperate pivot to salvage its core technology.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">No AI drama of this era unfolds without OpenAI — this one least of all. First, Sam Altman publicly backed Amodei’s principled stance — despite having refused to shake his hand at the AI summit in New Delhi just weeks earlier.</p><div Altman refuses to take the hand with Dario Amodei (third and second from the right, respectrively)</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">Then, immediately after the Pentagon branded Anthropic a supply-chain risk, Altman announced two things. OpenAI would step into the Pentagon contract. And it had secured a historic $110 billion funding round. Both on the same day. The entire Anthropic-Pentagon narrative was instantly subsumed under a single name: Sam Altman.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">OpenAI’s sudden alignment with the military becomes legible against this backdrop. In late 2025, the administration had explicitly refused a direct bailout — as there were enough frontier companies, and they were allowed to fail. OpenAI was out of options.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><span>By taking the Pentagon contract OpenAI established a new state-secured monopoly. And that is, essentially,</span><strong> a bailout that isn’t a bailout</strong><span>.</span></p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><span>Yet Altman wouldn’t be Altman if Anthropic were simply thrown under the bus. In the end, Anthropic may have been the biggest winner of the entire spectacle. Anthropic’s refusal is being celebrated as a </span><a href="https://substack.com/redirect/dc66a589-211c-4758-b6fc-567c1542654f?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg" rel="" style="color: rgb(54,55,55);text-decoration: underline;">second coming of Apple’s</a><span> famous decision not to install backdoors on its devices. </span><a href="https://substack.com/redirect/7b2c0410-2dbd-450e-9ef6-143710d992c0?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg" rel="" style="color: rgb(54,55,55);text-decoration: underline;">Downloads surged across</a><span> every app store. Millions switched from ChatGPT to Claude.</span></p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><span>What began as a meme quickly became a measurable exodus. Under the banner of #</span><a href="https://substack.com/redirect/eb0b3741-a64d-4da9-bf64-a9d35678b2f3?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg" rel="" style="color: rgb(54,55,55);text-decoration: underline;">QuitGPT</a><span>, over 1.5 million users pledged to boycott OpenAI, driving a 295% spike in ChatGPT uninstalls and propelling Claude to number one on the App Store.</span></p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><span>Amodei was declared a hero. The Atlantic even published an </span><a href="https://substack.com/redirect/8d1b1374-f4b0-4494-97a4-9a77c51e15c1?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg" rel="" style="color: rgb(54,55,55);text-decoration: underline;">article</a><span>, directly comparing Dario Amodei with Oppenheimer. </span></p><div class="captioned-image-container-static" style="font-size: 16px;line-height: 26px;margin: 32px auto;"><figure style="width: 100%;margin: 0 auto;"><table class="image-wrapper" width="100%" border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" the CEO of Anthropic, Dario Amodei</span></figcaption></figure></div><div style="font-size: 16px;line-height: 26px;"><hr style="margin: 32px 0;padding: 0;height: 1px;background: rgb(0,0,0,.1);border: none;"></div><h1 class="header-anchor-post" style="position: relative;font-family: 'SF Pro Display',-apple-system-headline,system-ui,-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,'Segoe UI',Roboto,Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif,'Apple Color Emoji','Segoe UI Emoji','Segoe UI Symbol';font-weight: bold;-webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;-moz-osx-font-smoothing: antialiased;-webkit-appearance: optimizelegibility;-moz-appearance: optimizelegibility;appearance: optimizelegibility;margin: 1em 0 0.625em 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 1.16em;font-size: 2em;">Act III. The Absence of the Human</h1><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">And yet, on this whole stage, there is but one reality: none of it truly matters.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><span>OpenAI may well be glad to shed its freeloaders — the free user base was nearly impossible to monetize. But even beyond such speculation: most consumer AI subscriptions are massively subsidized — by venture capital, by Big Tech, by one of the largest corporate subsidization campaigns in the history of technology. Even the most expensive Claude Max Subscription </span><a href="https://substack.com/redirect/b11e6f92-cc26-4b3a-8ec5-5f8bcce0f1c6?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg" rel="" style="color: rgb(54,55,55);text-decoration: underline;">may consume</a><span> up to $5.000 worth of compute.   </span></p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">It has never been about short-term profit, as none of these companies can cover their astronomical computing costs even remotely. The true goal is infrastructure capture. They want to be the default platform—meaning their models become the foundational layer that every other company, developer, and government agency is forced to build upon. They are giving away billions in subsidized compute today to ensure they own the monopolies of tomorrow. This is the field on which the entire theatre takes place.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">In this play, there can only be one winner: technology itself. And the principal loser is conspicuous by absence: The Human. In no act of this spectacle did The Human ever appear as subject. The Pentagon was only about securing its perceived dominance. OpenAI fought merely to survive its debt web.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><span>And Anthropic? The ethical AI company — which assembled its training data from the </span><a href="https://substack.com/redirect/d36db786-11a0-4f86-ba24-68ac990053b3?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg" rel="" style="color: rgb(54,55,55);text-decoration: underline;">intellectual property of the world</a><span>, while only a privileged few creators can afford the legal battle against such a giant. Before the Pentagon drama, Anthropic had already quietly </span><a href="https://substack.com/redirect/14248f4a-561f-40ac-b9a5-eeddc3d55cfb?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg" rel="" style="color: rgb(54,55,55);text-decoration: underline;">lowered the safety requirements</a><span> for its next generation of models.</span></p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><span>And the red lines? The recent mass killing of at least 160 schoolgirls during the strike on Iran was probably </span><a href="https://substack.com/redirect/53986821-3378-4bb1-bab0-e5ea1a14e33a?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg" rel="" style="color: rgb(54,55,55);text-decoration: underline;">caused by a faulty target identification</a><span> by Claude’s models. This happened one day after Amodei had been celebrated as the ethical conscience of the AI industry.</span></p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><span>Anthropic is far closer to OpenAI in spirit than either would ever admit. They need each other. One is the villain whose presence lets the other’s virtue shine. “</span><em>Thank God there’s only one Sam Altman!</em><span>” critics may say — while both companies jointly industrialize what Günther Anders once diagnosed as the ground condition of our epoch: the obsolescence of the human in the face of its own creations.</span></p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">If all of this is theatre, if the bailout is no bailout, then the play functions not as an attack on the human, which would presuppose intent, but as something staged past the human entirely, in complete structural indifference. </p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">The consumer’s decision to switch to Anthropic changes nothing about the underlying dynamic, even though it feels like resistance. The hand still reaches. What has been replaced is everything behind it.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">The true achievement of this spectacle: it takes the genuine moral outrage of its users, their still-functioning ethical intuition, and converts it into a new subscription. The Industrialization of Hype. In this apparatus, Anthropic can never be seen as the antidote. It is merely other side of the same mechanism. What it produces is the ultimate obsolescence: our deepest moral convictions, reduced to raw material for the expansion of the very system they were meant to oppose.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">In the end, the entire spectacle of AI ethics revealed itself as what it was from the start: a mechanism for securing backdoor bailouts — and for accelerating the very machinery that no longer requires the human it was supposedly about.</p><p class="button-wrapper" message.subject: 21 Reinforcement Learning (RL) Concepts Explained Simply
message.snippet: #130: Finally understand RL without getting confused by its fancy math equations ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏
message.plain_text_body:
View this post on the web at https://newsletter.systemdesign.one/p/what-is-reinforcement-learning

Get my system design playbook for FREE on newsletter signup:
Share this post [ https://substack.com/redirect/515c83f0-7c60-4852-8957-042a72e3cc12?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg ] & I'll send you some rewards for the referrals.
Reinforcement learning (RL) is making a comeback and becoming mainstream.
From humanoid robots and game-playing AIs that beat world champions to the LLMs you chat with every day, all are trained using RL, which lets them learn from experience and get better through feedback.
Contrary to many tutorials that can make it look so hard, RL is actually pretty intuitive, and you really don’t need a PhD to understand how it works.
In this newsletter, we will go through 21 key RL concepts to build a solid foundation from the ground up. These terms are all explained in plain language without using any fancy math equations.
Let’s start from the very beginning…
Want to reduce AI coding bugs before they hit production? (Partner) [ https://substack.com/redirect/49f517d1-ddc3-4bbb-bb74-95efb6358c92?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg ]
AI is writing more production code than ever, but it also introduces more risk.
CodeRabbit’s research found that AI-generated code contains 1.7x more issues, including 75% more logic errors.
That’s exactly why you should grab a copy of the Ultimate Prompting Cheat Sheet [ https://substack.com/redirect/49f517d1-ddc3-4bbb-bb74-95efb6358c92?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg ].
Here’s what you get:
Proven prompting techniques to reduce AI coding errors.
Actionable workflows for AI-assisted development.
Clear frameworks for safer AI-generated code.
Practical examples you can apply immediately.
And many more!
These techniques reduce defects without slowing velocity, regardless of which AI coding tool you use.
Sign up and get access to the ultimate prompting cheat sheet right now:
(Thanks to CodeRabbit for partnering on this post & sharing the ultimate prompting cheat sheet [ https://substack.com/redirect/49f517d1-ddc3-4bbb-bb74-95efb6358c92?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg ].)
I want to reintroduce Ashish Bamania [ https://substack.com/redirect/f2523ca5-6df0-48b5-97bb-20ddd53bddcd?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg ] as a guest author.
He’s a self-taught software engineer and an emergency physician. He is also the editor and primary author of the newsletters Into AI [ https://substack.com/redirect/4f4b03bb-a8d1-4905-9d90-60de3a83967b?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg ] and Into Quantum [ https://substack.com/redirect/56313cc2-2fa2-4104-a0a5-448941d44c91?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg ].
The images used in this newsletter come from his books:
LLMs In 100 Images [ https://substack.com/redirect/0fd1b533-df5b-47a0-a8f9-8c32283c0a2b?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg ]
Reinforcement Learning in 100 Images (upcoming)
Grab the book at 20% discount today with the discount code NEO20 [ https://substack.com/redirect/0fd1b533-df5b-47a0-a8f9-8c32283c0a2b?j=eyJ1IjoiM28yYngifQ.lK_fWvTbokdbQgO91mTS9lmdiqT7y_r7zXBY_yDFDTg ], pre-applied to the link above!
1. What is Reinforcement learning?
Let’s start with the definition of Reinforcement Learning (RL).
RL is a type of machine learning (besides Supervised and Unsupervised learning) that deals with an entity called an ‘Agent’, trying to learn to perform a task better in its ‘Environment’ through trial and error.
For example, a deer (Agent) foraging in a forest (Environment) to survive, avoiding being eaten by its predators.
We will soon move to more AI-related examples, I promise, but first, let’s better understand what the terms ‘Agent’ and ‘Environment’ mean.
2. Agent
An agent is the central entity in RL.
It is one that studies and interacts with its environment, makes decisions, takes actions, and learns from its outcomes.
In our previous case, we used a deer as an agent example, but from now on, we will discuss concepts assuming an LLM as the agent as well.
3. Environment
The environment is everything outside the agent with which it interacts.
The function of an environment is to:
Get affected by an agent’s actions
Change its state (or maybe hold its state constant) depending on the agent’s actions
Give the agent a reward or punishment based on its actions, so that the agent can modify its intent the next time it takes an action
In the first case, the deer was functioning in a forest as its environment. Based on the deer’s actions (moving around in different directions), either the forest (environment) lets it progress towards food (reward) or it exposes it to a cheetah (punishment).
In the case of an LLM as an agent, everything outside it, such as the following, is part of its environment:
User inputs
System prompt
Tools / APIs it can call
System responses (tool results, API outputs, error messages)
Context (documents, conversation history, files)
4. State
A State is a snapshot of an environment at a time. It is everything an agent sees at a given moment that it can use to make its next decision.
For a deer agent, it could be its current location in the forest, nearby predators, and the time of day.
For an LLM agent, it is all the context it has access to at a moment, which it can use to decide its next action.
But what’s action?
5. Action...

Unsubscribe https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9uZXdzbGV0dGVyLnN5c3RlbWRlc2lnbi5vbmUvYWN0aW9uL2Rpc2FibGVfZW1haWw_.rDt-PH2rVtO80w3PEQoNdoh6iCaRH8bo7viysrFHjRQ?

What is Reinforcement learning?</strong></h3><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><span>Let’s start with the definition of Reinforcement Learning (</span><strong>RL</strong><span>).</span></p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">RL is a type of machine learning (besides Supervised and Unsupervised learning) that deals with an entity called an ‘Agent’, trying to learn to perform a task better in its ‘Environment’ through trial and error.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">For example, a deer (Agent) foraging in a forest (Environment) to survive, avoiding being eaten by its predators.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">We will soon move to more AI-related examples, I promise, but first, let’s better understand what the terms ‘Agent’ and ‘Environment’ mean.</p><div class="captioned-image-container-static" style="font-size: 16px;line-height: 26px;margin: 32px auto;"><figure style="width: 100%;margin: 0 auto;"><table class="image-wrapper" Agent</strong></h3><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">An agent is the central entity in RL.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">It is one that studies and interacts with its environment, makes decisions, takes actions, and learns from its outcomes.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">In our previous case, we used a deer as an agent example, but from now on, we will discuss concepts assuming an LLM as the agent as well.</p><div class="captioned-image-container-static" style="font-size: 16px;line-height: 26px;margin: 32px auto;"><figure style="width: 100%;margin: 0 auto;"><table class="image-wrapper" 1.375em;"><strong>3. Environment</strong></h3><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">The environment is everything outside the agent with which it interacts.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">The function of an environment is to:</p><ul style="margin-top: 0;padding: 0;"><li style="margin: 8px 0 0 32px;mso-special-format: bullet;"><p style="color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;margin-bottom: 0;box-sizing: border-box;padding-left: 4px;font-size: 16px;margin: 0;">Get affected by an agent’s actions</p></li><li style="margin: 8px 0 0 32px;mso-special-format: bullet;"><p style="color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;margin-bottom: 0;box-sizing: border-box;padding-left: 4px;font-size: 16px;margin: 0;">Change its state (or maybe hold its state constant) depending on the agent’s actions</p></li><li style="margin: 8px 0 0 32px;mso-special-format: bullet;"><p style="color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;margin-bottom: 0;box-sizing: border-box;padding-left: 4px;font-size: 16px;margin: 0;">Give the agent a reward or punishment based on its actions, so that the agent can modify its intent the next time it takes an action</p></li></ul><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">In the first case, the deer was functioning in a forest as its environment. Based on the deer’s actions (moving around in different directions), either the forest (environment) lets it progress towards food (reward) or it exposes it to a cheetah (punishment).</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">In the case of an LLM as an agent, everything outside it, such as the following, is part of its environment:</p><ul style="margin-top: 0;padding: 0;"><li style="margin: 8px 0 0 32px;mso-special-format: bullet;"><p style="color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;margin-bottom: 0;box-sizing: border-box;padding-left: 4px;font-size: 16px;margin: 0;">User inputs</p></li><li style="margin: 8px 0 0 32px;mso-special-format: bullet;"><p style="color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;margin-bottom: 0;box-sizing: 26px;margin-bottom: 0;box-sizing: border-box;padding-left: 4px;font-size: 16px;margin: 0;">Tools / APIs it can call</p></li><li style="margin: 8px 0 0 32px;mso-special-format: bullet;"><p State</strong></h3><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">A State is a snapshot of an environment at a time. it could be its current location in the forest, nearby predators, and the time of day.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;">For an LLM agent, it is all the context it has access to at a moment, which it can use to decide its next action.</p><p style="margin: 0 0 20px 0;color: rgb(54,55,55);line-height: 26px;font-size: 16px;"><em>But what’s
